what_it_does: Defines a React canvas-based mask editor that loads a source image, lets the user paint transparent edit regions onto an offscreen mask canvas, previews those regions with a gold tint, and emits a PNG blob whose alpha channel encodes the mask.
observations: Uses two canvases: a visible composed canvas for image plus tint preview and a hidden mask canvas for alpha data.
Initializes the mask canvas as fully opaque so brush strokes remove alpha to mark editable regions.
The brush tool uses destination-out to create transparent mask areas; the eraser tool uses source-over to restore opaque mask areas.
repaintComposed reads mask pixel data, builds a temporary tint canvas for preview, updates hasMask, and exports a PNG blob through onChange.
The image is loaded with crossOrigin set to anonymous.
Display dimensions are derived from the requested height and source image aspect ratio, while canvas backing dimensions use the image natural size.
The UI exposes brush and eraser tools, brush-size range input, invert, reset, status text, and help text.
The component is assigned to window.MaskEditor.
standouts: Alpha semantics are inverted from ordinary painting: transparent mask pixels indicate the area to edit.
The preview tint is generated from the hidden mask alpha rather than stored as a separate drawing layer.
The exported PNG preserves source RGB data while replacing alpha with mask-derived edit transparency.
conventions: Uses React hooks destructured from the global React object with file-local aliases.
Keeps helper functions inside the component so they close over refs, state, and props.
Uses imperative canvas APIs for image composition, pixel inspection, and PNG export.
Exposes the component globally on window instead of using an ES module export.
Uses CSS class names prefixed with cv-mask for the component UI.
concerns: The header says onChange is debounced, but this file calls out.toBlob and onChange from repaintComposed without a debounce.
repaintComposed allocates a temporary canvas and scans full image pixel data on every repaint, including pointer movement.
onPointer calls repaintComposed during drawing, which also triggers blob export while the pointer is moving.
Pointer capture is not used, so drawing state depends on pointer up and leave events from the canvas element.
notes: This file is a self-contained React canvas mask editor that converts user brush strokes into alpha-channel edit masks with a tinted preview.
questions: Should mask export happen continuously during pointer movement or only after a committed stroke?
Is the missing debounce intentional despite the file header describing debounced output?
Should touch and stylus pointer behavior use pointer capture for drag continuity?
What guarantees that CvIcon and React are available globally before this file runs?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it implements a canvas brush mask editor and exports PNG alpha masks, but the stated debounced onChange behavior is not present in this file.
intention_signals: It appears intended to give users a compact in-browser mask painting tool for image edit workflows where transparent pixels identify the region to modify.
novelty: The file treats the hidden mask canvas as the source of truth and derives both the visible gold edit overlay and the emitted PNG alpha channel from that same alpha data.
contribution: Contributes a usable image mask editing substrate with brush and eraser interaction, alpha-based mask inversion and reset, visible edit-region feedback, and PNG blob output suitable for downstream image editing requests.
summary_for_embedding: A React MaskEditor component that loads an image, lets users paint transparent alpha regions on a hidden canvas mask, previews those regions with gold tint, and emits a source-sized PNG mask blob.
intention_for_embedding: This was for interactive creation of image edit masks where brush strokes define transparent edit areas while preserving the source image dimensions and RGB content.

symbols.does: Defines the React mask editor component and its UI, refs, state, image loading, canvas setup, and global exposure.
symbols.description: MaskEditor receives a source image, display height, and onChange callback. It maintains tool, brush size, drawing state, mask presence, dimensions, and canvas refs while rendering toolbar controls, the visible canvas, the hidden mask canvas, and status text.

symbols.does: Rebuilds the visible canvas preview from the source image and mask alpha, updates mask status, and emits a PNG blob.
symbols.description: This function redraws the source image, scans the hidden mask canvas for transparent pixels, creates a gold-tinted overlay for those edit regions, and sets hasMask based on whether any edit pixels exist. When onChange is present, it creates an output canvas with source RGB data and alpha set to 0 for edit regions or 255 elsewhere, then converts it to a PNG blob.

symbols.does: Handles pointer drawing by mapping screen coordinates to mask-canvas coordinates and painting a brush or eraser stroke.
symbols.description: onPointer computes canvas-space coordinates from the pointer event and visible canvas bounds, configures stroke style and line width, and uses destination-out for brush strokes or source-over for eraser strokes. It draws either an initial dot-like segment or a line from the previous pointer position, stores the last position, and repaints the composed preview.

symbols.does: Inverts mask alpha values or clears the whole mask when no transparent pixels are present.
symbols.description: invert reads the hidden mask canvas pixel data and checks whether any alpha differs from fully opaque. If the mask appears untouched, it clears the mask so the whole image becomes editable; otherwise it replaces each alpha value with 255 minus its current value, writes the data back, and repaints.

symbols.does: Resets the hidden mask canvas to fully opaque and repaints the preview.
symbols.description: clear clears the mask canvas, switches composition back to source-over, fills the entire mask with opaque black, and calls repaintComposed. In this file's mask semantics, that returns the editor to a no-edit-region state.
